topic: Exponential-tail, dependency-adaptive nonasymptotic confidence interval for a design-based causal exposure effect under general network interference: for the Horvitz-Thompson exposure estimator theta_hat of an exposure contrast theta=(1/n)sum_i[Y_i(a)-Y_i(b)] under KNOWN Bernoulli assignment with a neighborhood-dependent exposure mapping c_i=g(Z_{N(i)}) and known exposure propensities pi_i(.)>=pi_exp, construct a Holder-normalized fractional-coloring Bernstein e-value over an exact b-fold coloring of the exposure-dependency graph D (i~j iff N(i) cap N(j) nonempty), giving a finite-sample CI |theta_hat-theta| <= sqrt(2 chi_f(D) Vbar log(2/alpha)/n) + chi_f(D) log(2/alpha)/(3 n pi_exp) with observable propensity-only envelope Vbar=n^{-1}sum_i[1/pi_i(a)+1/pi_i(b)], whose half-width tracks the effective independent-exposure count n/chi_f(D) with log(1/alpha) tails -- the first exponential-tail, chi_f-adaptive, general-network finite-sample CI for exposure-HT inference (improving Aronow-Samii's Chebyshev CI), the network-interference lift of the negative-dependence effective-sample-size idea of Sandoval-Balakrishnan-Feller-Jordan-Waudby-Smith 2601.11744

open_problems.open_problem: Does the design-based Horvitz-Thompson contrast for a known network exposure mapping admit a finite-sample Bernstein confidence interval whose dependence penalty is chi_f(D), and is its n/chi_f(D) effective-sample-size scaling minimax for bounded potential outcomes?

source_refs.claim: Provides unbiased inverse-probability estimators for exposure effects under arbitrary known interference, with feasible inference based on conservative randomization variance and local-dependence asymptotics rather than chi_f-adaptive exponential tails.
source_refs.author: Peter M. Aronow and Cyrus Samii

source_refs.claim: Recovers asymptotic effective-sample-size scaling for nonasymptotic treatment-effect intervals using negative dependence or variance adaptivity, and proves information-theoretic lower bounds, but does not treat graph-dependent network exposures.
source_refs.author: Ricardo J. Sandoval, Sivaraman Balakrishnan, Avi Feller, Michael I. Jordan, and Ian Waudby-Smith

source_refs.claim: Constructs exact finite-sample intervals under two-stage randomization, but for binary outcomes and partial or stratified interference rather than general neighborhood exposure graphs.
source_refs.author: Joseph Rigdon and Michael G. Hudgens

source_refs.excerpt: replace the Wald-normal route with a finite-sample conservative inequality and a nontrivial minimality/sharpness
does not establish estimator-class optimality or practical tightness

open_problems.why_unsolved: Aronow-Samii and the banked ego-cluster work reduce exposure-HT inference to locally dependent inverse-propensity summands, but their feasible coverage routes are conservative-variance plus Chebyshev or asymptotic normal approximation. Sandoval et al. recover the right effective sample size by exploiting assignment dependence in ordinary randomized experiments, while fractional-coloring concentration controls generic dependency graphs; neither result handles the simultaneous inverse-exposure weights, overlap graph, and unobserved finite-population outcomes. A sharp result also needs a lower bound over all uniformly randomization-valid intervals, not merely a comparison within Wald intervals.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A normalized b-fold-color Holder/Bernstein e-value and inverted two-sided finite-population CI with leading width sqrt(chi_f(D) Vbar log(1/alpha)/n), plus a graph-regime minimax expected-length lower bound matching the n/chi_f(D) and propensity scaling.

writing_exemplar.why: Its design-to-exposure-mapping-to-estimand narrative is the canonical framing for the exact HT target being strengthened.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: randomization identifies exposure-specific potential-outcome means -> HT weighting handles unequal exposure probabilities -> general-network inference currently uses conservative variance and weak or asymptotic tails -> obtain an exponential-tail chi_f-adaptive finite-sample interval

method_exemplars.technique: Apply the dependency-graph Holder product inequality over an exact b-fold coloring and optimize the resulting moment-generating-function bound.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It directly converts independent-set exponential moments into a fractional-chromatic concentration bound, the mathematical object required by the proposed CI.

method_exemplars.technique: Construct propensity-sensitive nonasymptotic treatment-effect intervals through negative-dependence and variance-adaptive exponential bounds, then prove matching information-theoretic rate limits.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It solves the same effective-sample-size loss outside interference and supplies both the attainable-CI and minimax-lower-bound proof templates.

method_exemplars.technique: Decompose partly dependent sums into independent color classes and derive Hoeffding- and Bernstein-type large-deviation inequalities.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Its dependency-graph large-deviation argument is the foundational fixed-time concentration route to be specialized to exposure-HT summands.

open_problems.open_problem: Under heterogeneous Bernoulli assignment and approximate neighborhood interference, what joint decay, exposure-overlap, positivity, and variance regime permits an honest finite-sample exponential interval after adaptively truncating the exposure neighborhood?

source_refs.claim: Introduces approximate neighborhood interference, allowing distant treatments to have diminishing effects, and proves consistency and asymptotic normality with network-HAC inference rather than finite-sample exponential coverage.
source_refs.author: Michael P. Leung

source_refs.claim: Shows that rates under unknown limited interference depend on the amount of interference and its alignment with assignment dependence, and warns that conventional confidence statements may be inaccurate.
source_refs.author: Fredrik Savje, Peter M. Aronow, and Michael G. Hudgens

source_refs.claim: Its exposure estimator is exactly design-unbiased when the stated exposure mapping is correct, leaving approximate or misspecified hard-neighborhood mappings outside that finite-population guarantee.
source_refs.author: Peter M. Aronow and Cyrus Samii

source_refs.excerpt: Try weakening bounded overlap-dependency to a growing local-dependence condition closer to Aronow-Samii/Leung

open_problems.why_unsolved: Hard truncation produces a sparse exposure-dependency graph and hence useful fractional-color concentration, but it changes the exposure target whenever distant assignments retain nonzero causal influence. Leung controls this approximation only through asymptotic decay and topology conditions, while the prior bipartite proposal assumes uniformly bounded overlap dependence to control inverse product-exposure weights and Hajek denominators. Choosing the radius from the data creates a further selection problem: the deterministic truncation bias and stochastic chi_f(D_r) term must hold simultaneously over radii.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite-population coverage theorem whose radius is an explicit decay-modulus bias bound plus a chi_f(D_r)-adaptive Bernstein term, with a simultaneous or sample-split radius rule and an oracle inequality over an explicit growing-overlap/positivity rate region.

writing_exemplar.why: It gives the clearest motivation for replacing an unrealistic hard interference radius by a quantitative approximation regime.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: hard neighborhood interference rules out all distant effects -> common interaction models violate that cutoff -> approximate neighborhood interference imposes decay instead -> quantify finite-sample truncation bias and exponential uncertainty jointly

method_exemplars.technique: Bound exposure-mapping approximation error through a distance-decay modulus and network topology, then balance neighborhood radius against dependence.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies the deterministic approximation component required by the proposed bias-plus-concentration interval.

method_exemplars.technique: Apply graph-dependent Holder concentration to the radius-r truncated exposure estimator and union or mix across candidate radii.
method_exemplars.why_closest: For each fixed radius the induced overlap graph is exactly the dependence object controlled by the fractional-color inequality.

method_exemplars.technique: Track maximum dependency degree, bounded inverse-propensity summands, and variance through a Stein normal-approximation rate calculation.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It is the closest growing-local-dependence rate template, even though the desired resolution replaces its asymptotic normal approximation by finite-sample exponential control.

open_problems.open_problem: Among randomized network designs identifying a specified exposure contrast, which design minimizes worst-case fractional-coloring Bernstein width, and is the resulting graph-and-propensity objective a constant-factor surrogate for minimax confidence length?

source_refs.claim: Constructs a conflict-graph design for arbitrary neighborhood interference, obtains variance of order lambda(H)/n, and gives asymptotically valid intervals rather than an exponential-tail width criterion.
source_refs.author: Vardis Kandiros, Charilaos Pipis, Constantinos Daskalakis, and Christopher Harshaw

source_refs.claim: Optimizes participant selection and assignment in two-wave network experiments for estimator variance and proves regret and asymptotic inference guarantees, not finite-sample exponential-width guarantees.
source_refs.author: Davide Viviano

source_refs.claim: Proves information-theoretic optimality for effective-sample-size-adaptive nonasymptotic intervals in randomized experiments without network exposure dependence.
source_refs.author: Ricardo J. Sandoval, Sivaraman Balakrishnan, Avi Feller, Michael I. Jordan, and Ian Waudby-Smith

source_refs.excerpt: Investigate whether V_env is sharp or constant-factor sharp as a worst-case bounded-outcome variance envelope

open_problems.why_unsolved: Current network-design papers optimize variance, mean-squared error, or a spectral conflict-graph quantity and then invoke asymptotic inference. A nonasymptotic width objective couples two effects that those criteria separate: raising exposure propensities shrinks inverse weights but can create more dependence among exposure indicators, increasing chi_f(D). The banked heterogeneous-Bernoulli design optimizes only a conservative graph envelope, whose exact or constant-factor relation to worst-case bounded-outcome variance is itself unresolved; consequently envelope-optimality need not imply minimax confidence length.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An optimal-design or approximation theorem for minimizing chi_f(D_design) times an observable propensity envelope under budget and positivity constraints, together with either a constant-factor comparison to worst-case randomization-valid confidence length or a graph-sequence counterexample and matching lower bound.

writing_exemplar.why: Its conflict-graph framing makes the causal estimand, exposure incompatibilities, and graph-aware design objective explicit.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: interference makes target exposure states difficult to observe jointly -> a conflict graph encodes estimand-specific incompatibility -> assignment design can improve variance rates -> choose and certify the design that minimizes finite-sample exponential confidence width

method_exemplars.technique: Construct assignment distributions from an estimand-specific conflict graph and prove modified HT variance bounds through spectral graph structure.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It is the nearest design construction; the desired resolution replaces its spectral asymptotic-variance objective by a fractional-color exponential-width objective.

method_exemplars.technique: Optimize estimable quadratic variance upper bounds by convex programming and certify minimal conservativeness within the admissible class.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Its dual certificates are the closest machinery for deciding whether a tractable graph-only width envelope is sharp or only conservative.

method_exemplars.technique: Build difficult finite-population instances to lower-bound all nonasymptotic randomized-experiment confidence widths.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The same information-theoretic architecture can test whether the chi_f and exposure-propensity factors are unavoidable in network designs.

open_problems.open_problem: Can an empirically variance-adaptive exponential confidence interval for a network exposure-HT contrast remain uniformly design-valid when some joint exposure probabilities are zero?

source_refs.claim: Shows that unbiased or consistent design-based variance estimators generally do not exist under interference and characterizes minimally conservative estimable quadratic variance bounds by convex optimization.
source_refs.author: Christopher Harshaw, Joel A. Middleton, and Fredrik Savje
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: Explains that zero joint exposure probabilities obstruct unbiased variance estimation and therefore uses conservative design-based approximations.
source_refs.author: Peter M. Aronow and Cyrus Samii

source_refs.claim: Derives variance-adaptive betting confidence intervals and sequences for bounded means, including sampling without replacement, but not graph-dependent exposure-weighted causal estimators.
source_refs.author: Ian Waudby-Smith and Aaditya Ramdas
source_refs.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society Series B

open_problems.why_unsolved: Empirical Bernstein methods need an observable process that upper-controls tail-relevant variance, but under interference mutually incompatible exposure potential outcomes are never jointly observed. Optimized quadratic bounds are conservative in design expectation, whereas betting bounds exploit realized or predictable variance under simpler dependence. A synthesis must show that optimizing or updating an observable variance surrogate cannot understate the fractionally colored exponential variance at any realization, especially when zero joint exposure probabilities make exact covariance terms nonestimable.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A theorem constructing an observable conservative variance process from estimable quadratic bounds and proving a fractional-coloring empirical-Bernstein e-value whose inverted finite-sample CI is uniformly design-valid and strictly sharper than the propensity-only envelope on a stated low-variance regime.

writing_exemplar.why: It cleanly frames unobservable design variance as an optimization over estimable conservative upper bounds.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: design variance contains unobservable potential-outcome cross-products -> interference and zero joint exposure probabilities worsen nonestimability -> optimized quadratic bounds reduce conservativeness -> convert those bounds into a variance-adaptive exponential interval

method_exemplars.technique: Represent admissible conservative variance bounds as quadratic forms and select the least conservative member through convex optimization.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies the observable variance surrogate that the desired empirical-Bernstein construction would need.

method_exemplars.technique: Build composite nonnegative supermartingales with predictable betting fractions and prove empirical-Bernstein confidence bounds by Ville's inequality.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It is the direct proof template for converting observable variance information into finite-sample exponential inference.

method_exemplars.technique: Use leave-assignment-out recomputation to form a conservative Neyman-jackknife variance estimator under interference.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Its recomputation-based object is an alternative observable candidate for the required self-normalization.

open_problems.open_problem: Can fractional-coloring e-values be made into a nonasymptotic confidence sequence for an exposure effect under sequential monitoring of network experiments without losing the n/chi_f effective sample size?

source_refs.claim: Develops design-based confidence sequences for sequential experiments, including bandit, time-series, and panel settings, but not general network exposure-graph inference.
source_refs.author: Dae Woong Ham, Iavor Bojinov, Michael Lindon, and Martin Tingley

source_refs.claim: Provides model-free sequential tests and confidence sequences for randomized treatment effects, but not graph-dependent neighborhood exposure effects.
source_refs.author: Michael Lindon, Dae Woong Ham, Martin Tingley, and Iavor Bojinov

source_refs.claim: Develops nonasymptotic time-uniform Bernstein and self-normalized confidence sequences from exponential supermartingales.
source_refs.author: Steven R. Howard, Aaditya Ramdas, Jon McAuliffe, and Jasjeet Sekhon

open_problems.why_unsolved: A fixed-network fractional Holder inequality controls one terminal product of exponential factors, but optional stopping requires a single process that is a supermartingale for the experiment's filtration. Sequential revelation creates within-look neighborhood dependence and across-look dependence through previous assignments or adaptive design choices. Existing design-based confidence sequences use martingale assignment structure without overlapping exposure graphs, while existing fractional-color concentration is fixed-time and does not supply predictable color weights.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A test-supermartingale or e-process indexed by the exposure contrast, with predictable fractional-color normalization and a stitched or mixture boundary giving time-uniform finite-population coverage and leading width of order sqrt(chi_f V_t loglog(V_t)/n_t).

writing_exemplar.why: Its continuous-monitoring motivation naturally explains why a fixed-time network interval is insufficient.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: online experiments are monitored continuously -> fixed-horizon intervals fail under data-dependent stopping -> design-based confidence sequences restore uniform validity -> extend that guarantee to graph-dependent causal exposures without discarding effective sample size

method_exemplars.technique: Mix or stitch exponential supermartingales over intrinsic-time scales and invert the resulting uniform boundary.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies the standard proof machinery needed to turn a fixed-time Bernstein bound into a confidence sequence.

method_exemplars.technique: Control products of graph-dependent random variables by Holder normalization over b-fold color classes.
method_exemplars.why_closest: This is the dependence-combination step that must be made compatible with the sequential filtration.

method_exemplars.technique: Use predictable betting strategies to obtain variance-adaptive confidence sequences for bounded observations.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Its betting construction is structurally closest to the proposed e-process apart from the missing network-dependence layer.

literature_map:
1. Aronow and Samii (2017, AOAS, arXiv:1305.6156) establish the exposure-mapping/IPW framework, conservative randomization variance, and local-dependence CLTs for arbitrary known interference.
2. Rigdon and Hudgens (2015, Statistics & Probability Letters, DOI 10.1016/j.spl.2015.06.011) give exact intervals only for binary outcomes under partial/stratified interference.
3. Savje, Aronow, and Hudgens (2021, Annals of Statistics, DOI 10.1214/20-AOS1973) tie rates under unknown interference to its alignment with assignment dependence and caution that conventional CIs can fail.
4. Leung (2022, Econometrica, DOI 10.3982/ECTA17841) replaces hard neighborhood cutoffs by approximate neighborhood interference, but inference remains network-HAC and asymptotic.
5. Kandiros, Pipis, Daskalakis, and Harshaw (2024, arXiv:2411.10908) design experiments through an estimand-specific conflict graph, obtaining spectral variance rates and asymptotic CIs.
6. Viviano (arXiv:2003.08421) optimizes two-wave network designs for variance and proves design-regret guarantees, not exponential-tail width guarantees.
7. Harshaw, Middleton, and Savje (JASA 2026, arXiv:2112.01709) show design variance is generally nonestimable and optimize conservative estimable quadratic bounds.
8. Park and Wager (2026, arXiv:2604.24017) propose a general Neyman-jackknife route to conservative variance estimation under interference.
9. Sandoval, Balakrishnan, Feller, Jordan, and Waudby-Smith (2026, arXiv:2601.11744) attain asymptotic effective-sample-size scaling with nonasymptotic intervals in ordinary randomized experiments.
10. Janson (2004, Random Structures & Algorithms, DOI 10.1002/rsa.20008) derives large deviations for dependency-graph sums by decomposing them into independent color classes.
11. Pelekis, Ramon, and Wang (2017, Indagationes Mathematicae, DOI 10.1016/j.indag.2016.11.017) prove the b-fold-color Holder inequality that exposes the fractional-chromatic dependence penalty.
12. Howard, Ramdas, McAuliffe, and Sekhon (2021, Annals of Statistics, arXiv:1810.08240) provide stitched and mixture Bernstein confidence-sequence machinery.
13. Waudby-Smith and Ramdas (2024, JRSSB, arXiv:2010.09686) construct variance-adaptive betting CIs and CSs for bounded means and sampling without replacement.
14. Ham, Bojinov, Lindon, and Tingley (arXiv:2210.08639), and Lindon et al. (arXiv:2210.08589), develop anytime-valid causal inference without general exposure-graph dependence.
15. The live tension is sharp: causal network papers offer unbiasedness, conservative variance, and asymptotics; concentration papers offer chi_f-sensitive exponential tails; recent causal e-process papers recover effective sample size or optional-stopping validity only outside general network exposure dependence.
prior_proposal_map:
Mined the adjacent downgraded exp_ego_cluster_exposure_clt / ego_cluster_polynomial_clt bank entry, including all three angle review JSONs and its bank README.
The highest-yield ego-cluster signal was angle0_v2 strengthening_paths[0], which explicitly requests a finite-sample conservative inequality with nontrivial sharpness in place of the Wald-normal route.
The ego-cluster bank README separately records that completion-envelope sharpness does not establish estimator-class optimality or practical tightness.
Mined the accepted exp_bipartite_minimax_design / v1 proposal bank, its README, and angle0_v1-v3 reviewer JSONs.
Bipartite angle0_v3 yielded two adjacent unresolved questions: growing local dependence compatible with denominator/CLT rates, and exact or constant-factor sharpness of the graph-only variance envelope.
Inspected the failed panel_approx_nbhd_interference proposal and its reviewer JSONs; its remaining issues concern ANI/NCR/randomization-procedure equivalence and were not promoted because they are less adjacent to finite-sample exposure-HT concentration.
Inspected the experimentation bank READMEs for exp_design_pm1_exactness_boundary, exp_rollout_chebyshev_minimax, and exp_saturation_skew_threshold; they concern design implementability or rollout/saturation optimization rather than fixed-design exposure-HT tail inference.
No adjacent *_proposal_angle*_rejected.tex files were present, and the active target qid contains only initialization/state artifacts, so it contributed no prior-proposal gap.
